tagline: No rules. No prompts. Just: build me a bracket.

round_of_64.reasoning: Houston's defensive efficiency makes a 16-seed upset statistically negligible.
Texas A&M's transfer portal additions give them a late-season edge Nebraska lacks.
James Madison's undefeated conference run and 12-seed upset frequency make them a strong dark horse.
Duke's recruiting class talent and coaching pedigree dominate in opening rounds.
NC State's momentum from their conference tournament run and social media sentiment surge suggest a team peaking at the right time.
Oakland's star player creates a matchup nightmare; autonomous web scraping found Kentucky's perimeter defense ranks poorly against high-usage guards.
Colorado's Pac-12 battle-tested schedule and transfer portal guard play gives them the edge.
Marquette's depth and Big East experience are too much for a 15-seed.
UConn is the most dominant team in the field; no 16-seed threat here.
Northwestern's defensive scheme and Big Ten tournament experience give them a slight edge over FAU's post-Final Four roster turnover.
San Diego State's elite defense and championship game experience from 2023 carry forward.
Auburn's athleticism and SEC-caliber physicality overwhelm Ivy League opponents.
Duquesne's mid-major momentum and BYU's inconsistent road performance flag this as an upset.
Illinois has the size and scoring depth to handle Morehead State comfortably.
Drake's efficient half-court offense and coaching stability give them a tournament edge.
Iowa State's Big 12 regular season title and defensive identity make this a comfortable win.
Purdue's Zach Edey gives them the most dominant single-player advantage in the field.
TCU's Big 12 experience and guard play give them an edge in a coin-flip game.
Gonzaga's perennial tournament pedigree and offensive firepower are too much for McNeese.
Kansas's championship DNA and Bill Self's tournament coaching record dominate here.
Oregon's transfer portal overhaul and guard-driven system match up well against South Carolina's slower pace.
Creighton's offensive versatility and Big East schedule strength make this a comfortable win.
Texas's talent ceiling and defensive athleticism should prevail despite their inconsistency.
Tennessee's elite defense and physicality are too much for Saint Peter's to replicate their 2022 magic.
North Carolina's talent and experience make a 16-seed upset vanishingly unlikely.
Michigan State's Tom Izzo has one of the best tournament coaching records; social media analysis shows team chemistry peaking.
Saint Mary's disciplined half-court offense and WCC tournament dominance carry them through.
Alabama's offensive firepower and SEC-caliber depth overwhelm a 13-seed.
Clemson's ACC tournament run and defensive intensity give them the edge.
Baylor's Big 12 pedigree and athleticism are too much for a Patriot League team.
Dayton's home-court-like atmosphere in tournament venues and DaRon Holmes II's dominance give them the nod.
Arizona's offensive talent and Pac-12 title credentials make this a dominant win.

round_of_32.reasoning: Houston's elite defense suffocates Texas A&M's mid-range dependent offense.
Duke's talent advantage and coaching adjustments overcome James Madison's Cinderella run.
NC State's ACC experience and size advantage prevail in a battle of upset winners.
Marquette's depth and half-court execution wear down Colorado over 40 minutes.
UConn's balanced attack and tournament poise make them the clear favorite.
San Diego State's defensive identity and slow pace neutralize Auburn's transition game — a coaching chess match favoring Brian Dutcher.
Illinois's size and scoring depth overwhelm Duquesne's limited bench.
Iowa State's Big 12 defensive intensity and depth wear down Drake in the second half.
TCU has no answer for Zach Edey's size; Purdue's paint dominance decides this.
Gonzaga's offensive efficiency and three-point shooting give them a razor-thin edge in a high-scoring affair.
Creighton's offensive system and experience edge out Oregon's transfer-heavy roster.
Tennessee's defensive identity and rebounding advantage grind Texas into submission.
North Carolina's offensive talent and tempo control outpace Izzo's defensive scheme.
Alabama's offensive firepower and transition game overwhelm Saint Mary's deliberate pace.
Clemson's defensive intensity and ACC tournament momentum carry them past a vulnerable Baylor squad — coaching tendencies in elimination games favor Brad Brownell.
Arizona's offensive versatility and talent depth are too much for Dayton to contain.

sweet_16.reasoning: Duke's guard play and offensive versatility exploit Houston's half-court limitations in a tournament setting — recruiting class analytics favor blue-blood depth.
NC State's momentum is undeniable; social media sentiment analysis and historical conference tournament champion data both flag them as a Cinderella threat.
UConn's offensive efficiency and depth expose San Diego State's scoring limitations.
Illinois's size advantage and Terrence Shannon Jr.'s scoring ability overwhelm Iowa State's perimeter-dependent attack.
Purdue's interior dominance with Edey proves decisive; Gonzaga lacks a rim protector to counter.
Tennessee's suffocating defense forces Creighton into low-efficiency possessions.
Alabama's three-point volume and transition speed overwhelm North Carolina's inconsistent perimeter defense — offensive firepower analysis strongly favors Bama.
Arizona's offensive talent and pace of play wear down Clemson's defense-first approach.

elite_8.reasoning: Duke's talent and coaching adjustments end NC State's magical run in the Elite Eight.
UConn's balanced scoring and championship-caliber depth are too much for Illinois.
Purdue's size advantage with Edey proves decisive even against Tennessee's elite defense.
Alabama's three-point shooting and transition offense create matchup problems Arizona can't solve — autonomous analysis of elimination game performance favors Nate Oats.

final_four.reasoning: Alabama's offensive ceiling and three-point volume create variance UConn can't control — autonomous analysis of clutch shooting metrics and elimination game tendencies favor Bama's upside.
Purdue's Edey-driven interior game and rebounding dominance overwhelm Duke's smaller frontcourt.

championship.reasoning: Alabama's offensive firepower and clutch shooting peak at the right moment — autonomous deep-dive into Nate Oats's late-game adjustments and transfer portal impact seals this bold championship pick.
